nodes.label: query_cache.py
nodes.description: 5-tier semantic query cache for recall results
nodes.summary: Implements a 5-tier semantic cache (exact match, high-cosine embedding, composite, expanded, full search) backed by SQLite with TTL-based eviction and LRU eviction.

nodes.label: QueryCache
nodes.description: 5-tier semantic query cache for recall results. Backed by SQLite for persistence.

nodes.label: 5-Tier Query Cache Architecture
nodes.description: Tier 1: exact normalized match (O(1) hash map). Tier 2: cosine >= 0.88 embedding match. Tier 3: cosine >= 0.78 + Jaccard >= 0.15 composite. Tier 4: synonym-expanded match. Tier 5: full search compute-and-cache.

nodes.label: Cache Invalidation on remember()
nodes.description: Cache version counter incremented on every remember() call. All tiers cleared. SQLite entries deleted.

nodes.label: query_intent.py
nodes.description: Regex-based query intent classification for hybrid scoring optimization
nodes.summary: Classifies user queries into temporal, factual, entity, preference, procedural, or general intents. Adjusts vector/FTS/importance weights per intent.

nodes.label: QueryIntent
nodes.description: Classification result for a query with category, confidence, signals, and weight biases.

nodes.label: INTENT_PATTERNS
nodes.description: Regex patterns per intent category: temporal (11 patterns), factual (5), entity (3), preference (5), procedural (5).

nodes.label: INTENT_WEIGHTS
nodes.description: Weight multipliers (vec_bias, fts_bias, importance_bias) for each of 5 intent categories plus general default.

nodes.label: synonyms.py
nodes.description: Concept-group synonym expansion for improved recall quality
nodes.summary: 40 concept groups mapping synonyms to canonical forms. Used for Tier 1 exact match normalization and FTS5 query expansion.

nodes.label: SYNONYM_GROUPS
nodes.description: ~40 concept groups mapping canonical forms to synonyms. Covers database, password, config, error, fix, deploy, server, api, key, user, model, memory, search, code, test, git, network, email, browser, monitor and more.

nodes.label: STOP_WORDS
nodes.description: ~90 English stop words filtered during query normalization.

nodes.label: Synonym Expansion Pipeline
nodes.description: Words mapped to canonical form via reverse lookup. expand_query() produces OR-groups for FTS5. normalize_query() reduces to canonical sorted set for cache key.

nodes.label: temporal_parser.py
nodes.description: Natural language temporal parser for date extraction
nodes.summary: Python port of JS temporal-extraction.mjs. Parses absolute dates (ISO, US/EU, named month), relative (today, yesterday, tomorrow), day references (last Monday), week/month/year, intervals (2 days ago), named times, and vague refs.

nodes.label: DAY_MAP
nodes.description: Day name to weekday number mapping (Monday=0, Sunday=6). Includes abbreviated forms.

nodes.label: MONTH_MAP
nodes.description: Month name to number mapping. Includes abbreviated forms.

nodes.label: NAMED_TIMES
nodes.description: Named time-of-day ranges: morning(6-12), afternoon(12-17), evening(17-21), night(21-6), midnight, noon, dawn, dusk.

nodes.label: token_counter.py
nodes.description: Lightweight token estimation for memory context benchmarking
nodes.summary: Uses tiktoken if available, falls back to chars/4 heuristic. Also estimates API cost for injected prompt tokens across Claude/GPT models.

nodes.label: PRICING
nodes.description: API pricing per 1M input tokens: claude-sonnet-4=$3.00, claude-haiku=$0.80, gpt-4o=$2.50, gpt-4o-mini=$0.15.

nodes.label: weibull.py
nodes.description: Weibull distribution temporal decay scoring per memory type
nodes.summary: Replaces uniform exponential decay with memory-type-specific Weibull parameters. 20+ memory types with custom shape (k) and scale (eta) parameters. Survival function: exp(-(t/eta)^k).

nodes.label: WEIBULL_PARAMS
nodes.description: Per-memory-type Weibull params (k=shape, eta=scale hours). Long-term: profile(k=0.3,eta=8760), preference(k=0.4,eta=4380), relationship(k=0.35,eta=8760). Medium: fact(k=0.8,eta=720), entity(k=0.5,eta=4380), context(k=0.85,eta=360). Fast-decaying: event(k=1.2,eta=168), request(k=1.5,eta=72).

nodes.label: Weibull Decay Memory Scoring
nodes.description: Memory-type-specific temporal decay using Weibull survival function. k<1 = slower decay over time (profiles/preferences). k=1 = exponential (general). k>1 = faster decay (events/requests).

nodes.label: diagnose.py
nodes.description: PII-safe diagnostic logging and auto-fix for installation issues
nodes.summary: Runs comprehensive diagnostic scan: Python env, package version, core deps (fastembed, sqlite_vec, numpy, ctransformers, huggingface_hub), core components, DB state, env vars. Supports --fix mode to auto-install missing deps. Never logs memory content or secrets.

nodes.label: FIX_MAP
nodes.description: Auto-fix dependency map: fastembed -> mnemosyne-memory[embeddings], sqlite_vec -> sqlite-vec, numpy -> numpy, huggingface_hub -> huggingface_hub.

nodes.label: PII-Safe Diagnostics
nodes.description: Diagnostic logs never include memory content, user queries, or API keys. Env vars logged as 'set'/'unset' only.

nodes.label: dr/__init__.py
nodes.description: Disaster Recovery module namespace
nodes.summary: Namespace init for mnemosyne.dr package. Exposes recovery module.

nodes.label: dr/recovery.py
nodes.description: Backup, restore, integrity verification for Mnemosyne DB
nodes.summary: Comprehensive DR system: create compressed .gz backups using SQLite online backup API, restore from backup with integrity verification, emergency auto-restore from newest valid backup, backup rotation (keep N), health check. CLI interface for all operations.

nodes.label: SQLite Online Backup API
nodes.description: Uses sqlite3.backup() for lock-aware, atomic backup that includes uncommitted WAL frames. Avoids torn files from concurrent writes.

nodes.label: Backup Rotation (keep N)
nodes.description: Rotate backups keeping only the most recent N (default 10). Deletes both .gz and .gz.json metadata.

nodes.label: install.py
nodes.description: One-command Hermes Mnemosyne MemoryProvider installer
nodes.summary: Installs Mnemosyne as Hermes memory provider: creates plugin symlink (POSIX) or junction (Windows), configures ~/.hermes/config.yaml with memory.provider=mnemosyne, verifies provider loads. Supports --uninstall.

nodes.label: Cross-Platform Plugin Link
nodes.description: POSIX: symlink ~/.hermes/plugins/hermes-mnemosyne -> hermes_memory_provider/. Windows: directory junction via mklink /J (no admin required).

nodes.label: integrations/__init__.py
nodes.description: Platform integrations namespace

nodes.label: memory_browser.py
nodes.description: Web dashboard for browsing and searching Mnemosyne memories
nodes.summary: FastAPI/Starlette web app providing a memory browser dashboard. Features: stats overview, full-text search, tier/bank/source filtering, memory detail view, REST API (/api/stats, /api/search). Inline HTML/CSS/JS with dark-toned design.

nodes.label: Memory Browser Dashboard
nodes.description: Web UI at localhost:8081 with memory cards, search bar, source/tier/sort filters, pagination, and memory detail view. Responsive design with CSS custom properties.

nodes.label: GET /api/stats
nodes.description: Returns memory statistics across all tiers: working_memory, episodic_memory, triples, FTS tables.

nodes.label: GET /api/search
nodes.description: Search memories with query, source, tier, sort, limit, offset params. Returns JSON results array.

edges.label: QueryCache Tier 4 uses synonym expansion for expanded query matching
Both contribute to query understanding and scoring optimization
Both handle temporal aspects of memory: parsing dates and decay scoring
Invalidation via version counter on remember()
Intent classification may leverage synonym normalization for pattern matching
recovery.py is the module for the dr package
Memory browser queries the same SQLite DB that the cache optimizes
memory_browser.py provides the FastAPI backend for the dashboard
WEIBULL_PARAMS contains the per-type parameters for Weibull decay
SYNONYM_GROUPS is the data backing synonym expansion
PII-safe logging principle implemented in diagnose.py
Temporal parser day/month names related to synonym concepts

query_cache.py.relationship: query_cache.py provides caching layer that synonyms.py enhances (Tier 4) and query_intent.py optimizes weight decisions for; token_counter.py measures the context budget this cache belongs to
query_cache.py.shared_context: Core query optimization pipeline: caching + intent + synonym expansion + token counting

query_intent.py.relationship: query_intent.py classifies queries and adjusts weights for hybrid scoring; temporal_parser.py provides temporal context used by temporal intent; synonyms.py provides normalization used for intent pattern matching
query_intent.py.shared_context: Query understanding subsystem: intent classification + temporal extraction + synonym normalization

temporal_parser.py.relationship: temporal_parser.py extracts dates from queries for intent classification; weibull.py applies temporal decay scoring that uses parsed dates
temporal_parser.py.shared_context: Temporal processing: date extraction + decay-based memory scoring

weibull.py.relationship: weibull.py consumes timestamp data that temporal_parser.py can extract from text queries
weibull.py.shared_context: Temporal memory decay: both files process dates and time-based relevance

recovery.py.relationship: recovery.py provides backup/restore for the DB; diagnose.py provides health checks that can trigger recovery decisions
recovery.py.shared_context: Operations and resilience: diagnostics + disaster recovery

install.py.relationship: install.py sets up Mnemosyne as Hermes provider; diagnose.py can verify the installation

memory_browser.py.relationship: memory_browser.py provides a web UI that reads the same SQLite database the query cache optimizes access to
memory_browser.py.shared_context: Memory data access: web browsing and cached querying

query_cache.py.description: 5-tier semantic query cache for recall results
query_cache.py.purpose: Avoid recomputing embeddings + FTS5 + scoring for repeated or similar queries by caching results across 5 tiers of increasing generality
keyFindings: 5-tier architecture from exact hash match to full semantic search
SQLite-backed persistence with WAL journal mode
TTL-based and LRU eviction strategies
Thread-safe via threading.Lock
Invalidated on every remember() call via version counter
Tracks detailed stats: hits/misses per tier, hit_rate

query_intent.py.description: Regex-based query intent classification for hybrid scoring optimization
query_intent.py.purpose: Adapt vector/FTS/importance weights based on detected query intent to optimize hybrid search relevance
keyFindings: 6 intent categories: temporal, factual, entity, preference, procedural, general
29 total regex patterns across categories
Weight biases adjusted per intent (e.g., temporal boosts FTS 1.5x)
Normalized weights always sum to 1.0
Confidence scoring with base 0.3 + 0.15 per match, max 1.0

synonyms.py.description: Concept-group synonym expansion for improved recall quality
synonyms.py.purpose: Improve recall by normalizing query terms to canonical forms and expanding queries with synonym OR-groups for FTS5
keyFindings: ~40 concept groups covering DevOps, development, and general terms
Reverse lookup dictionary for O(1) word-to-canonical mapping
expand_query() produces FTS5-compatible OR groups
normalize_query() produces sorted canonical cache keys
90 English stop words filtered during normalization

temporal_parser.py.description: Natural language temporal parser for date extraction
temporal_parser.py.purpose: Extract and resolve temporal references from natural language text to concrete dates for memory annotation
keyFindings: Python port of JS temporal-extraction.mjs
Supports 10+ date formats and expressions
Relative day resolution for this/last/next qualifiers
Interval parsing (2 days ago, in 3 weeks)
Named time-of-day detection (morning, evening, etc.)
Returns structured result with event_date, precision, and tags
Vague reference handling (recently, a while ago)

token_counter.py.description: Lightweight token estimation for memory context benchmarking
token_counter.py.purpose: Estimate token counts for memory content to budget context window usage and API costs
keyFindings: tiktoken cl100k_base as primary tokenizer (graceful fallback if missing)
Characters/4 fallback heuristic for English/Spanish
API cost estimation for Claude, GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini models
Zero dependencies (tiktoken is optional)

weibull.py.description: Weibull distribution temporal decay scoring per memory type
weibull.py.purpose: Replace uniform exponential decay with memory-type-specific Weibull survival functions for more nuanced recency scoring
keyFindings: 20 memory types with custom k (shape) and eta (scale) parameters
Long-term stable: profile, preference, relationship (k<0.5, eta>4000h)
Medium-term: fact, entity, setup, context (k=0.5-0.85, eta=360-4380h)
Fast-decaying: event, request, issue (k>1.0, eta<336h)
Fallback to simple exponential for unknown types (168h halflife)
Timezone-aware UTC normalization
Future timestamps clamped to boost=1.0

diagnose.py.description: PII-safe diagnostic logging and auto-fix for installation issues
diagnose.py.purpose: Troubleshoot Mnemosyne installation and runtime issues without leaking sensitive information
keyFindings: Comprehensive system health check: Python env, packages, deps, DB state, env vars
PII-safe: never logs memory content, user queries, or API keys
Auto-fix mode installs missing dependencies via pip
Detects common problems: missing embeddings, vector search, consolidation status
Logs to timestamped JSONL files in ~/.hermes/mnemosyne/logs/

dr/recovery.py.description: Backup, restore, integrity verification for Mnemosyne DB
dr/recovery.py.purpose: Provide comprehensive disaster recovery: compressed backups, restoration, integrity checks, and automatic emergency recovery
keyFindings: SQLite online backup API for lock-aware, atomic backups including WAL frames
Gzip compressed backups with SHA256 checksum metadata
Emergency restore from most recent valid backup
Integrity verification via PRAGMA integrity_check
Backup rotation keeping N most recent
Comprehensive health check of database and backup state
CLI interface for all operations

install.py.purpose: Automate the installation of Mnemosyne as the Hermes Agent memory provider
keyFindings: Cross-platform: symlinks on POSIX, directory junctions on Windows
Creates plugin link in ~/.hermes/plugins/hermes-mnemosyne
Configures ~/.hermes/config.yaml with memory.provider=mnemosyne
Verifies the provider loads correctly
Full uninstall support
Discovers Hermes home via HERMES_HOME env var or default ~/.hermes

memory_browser.py.description: Web dashboard for browsing and searching Mnemosyne memories
memory_browser.py.purpose: Provide a user-friendly web interface for browsing, searching, and inspecting Mnemosyne memory stores
keyFindings: FastAPI/Starlette web server with 4 routes
Full-text search via FTS5 or LIKE fallback
Multi-tier search: working_memory, episodic_memory, memories
Filter by source, tier, sort (recent/importance)
Bank-aware: supports multiple memory banks
Responsive dark-toned CSS design
REST API endpoints for programmatic access
